reviews.author: DarkStation
reviews.body: If you hadn’t had a chance to play it yet at all though, and your computer can handle it, the PC version does definitely feel like the definitive version.

reviews.author: CD-Action
reviews.body: Rockstar promised that the PC edition of GTA V would be the best way to experience this breathtaking game, and that’s exactly what it is. [06/2015, p.48]

reviews.author: SpazioGames
reviews.body: We could not expect a better PC port PC for Grand Theft Auto V. Rockstar North, offers right from day one a huge game in optimized, widely scalable, and high impact graphics and the video editor, although not so easy to use, is an enjoyable addition.

reviews.author: GameOver.gr
reviews.body: Beautiful, witty, rude and captious Rockstar’s GTA V PC release is a must play title. Entangling open world, a great variety in missions, tons of online content and a port that respects the game itself and PC gamers alike.

reviews.author: PC PowerPlay
reviews.body: Packed with a wide range of gameplay, some great features, and - amazingly - works the first time. Great stuff from Rockstar. [June 2015, p.53]

reviews.author: LEVEL (Czech Republic)
reviews.body: Even more free, prettier, smoother and in every aspect a more comfortable Los Santos than we all remember. Old song for the extra money you say? C'mon, for game like this you can never pay too much. [Issue#252]

reviews.author: Impulsegamer
reviews.body: Ultimately you will get everything you expected from GTA 5 for PC; a tremendous game with many months of playability and the potential to only get better.

reviews.author: DarkZero
reviews.body: At the end of the day, this is still Grand Theft Auto V, an entry that I find is the best in the series, and a game that has been made that little bit better with the performance boost and graphical enhancements.

reviews.author: Gamer.no
reviews.body: Quotation forthcoming.

reviews.author: CGMagazine
reviews.body: If GTAV was more fully devoted to being either a comedy or a drama, its narrative may have been more successful. As it is, the most fun to be had in the game comes almost entirely from the moments when the player breaks away from the mission structure and enjoys the world without worrying about the context of its plot.

reviews.author: Gaming Age
reviews.body: No doubt, future updates and optimizations will be patched in, but for what we've got right out of the gate, there's a lot going on for the PC crowd this time around.

reviews.author: Digitally Downloaded
reviews.body: It is sharp, witty, intelligent, and just the right shade of nasty -, and is a true inspiration for future open-world action adventure games.

reviews.author: Worth Playing
reviews.body: The PC version may not have any new content, but it hits the platform in an extremely polished way. It won't change your mind about the game if you've already played it on another platform, but it certainly cements itself as a fantastic version of an entertaining game.

reviews.author: ActionTrip
reviews.body: GTA V on the PC is graphically beautiful and runs like half-melted butter.

reviews.author: AusGamers
reviews.body: Sure, the Rockstar Social Club is an annoying hoop to jump through, and hardware fragmentation poses problems for some. But for those with the hardware to run it, GTA V serves as a testament to the PC platform's superiority, and a reminder that good things come to those that wait.

reviews.author: NZGamer
reviews.body: If you’re over 18, and you’re not sensitive to what I’ve mentioned before, I can’t recommend this game enough. It’s an amazing look at how far ahead of the game Rockstar are with their open-world titles, and their living, breathing environments.

reviews.author: Dealspwn
reviews.body: Fantastic visuals and enhanced AI behaviour makes the virtual world of San Andreas come to life, and the Rockstar Editor is a powerful tool for machinima creators. It really is the definitive version of a modern classic.

reviews.author: Hobby Consolas
reviews.body: This is the best version available of one of the greatest games in History. GTA V for PC is almost perfect, if your PC is powerful enough to run the game in 4K resolution, at 60 FPS.

reviews.author: Ragequit.gr
reviews.body: GTA V PC is the ultimate version of one of the greatest games in history and the best open-world game that you will play until the advent of GTA VI or RDR 2.

reviews.author: Games.cz
reviews.body: GTA V's PC version is the true mother of all versions of the epic crime adventure. Great optimization and controls adjusted for PC gameplay show that quality conversions still have not died.

reviews.author: Critical Hit
reviews.body: Grand Theft Auto V is at home on PC, with Rockstar pouring an incredible amount of effort and work into their long overdue PC port. There is simply no better way to experience the branching story in Los Santos - setting a new benchmark on what we can expect from the highest tier of visual fidelity in gaming.

reviews.author: We Got This Covered
reviews.body: Even on its third outing, Grand Theft Auto V continually manages to impress and astound, and the refinements made on the PC version are just the icing on the cake.

reviews.author: Gameplanet
reviews.body: There is simply no argument to be had. The PC release of GTA V is by far the very best version of the very best Grand Theft Auto game to date.

reviews.author: Digital Chumps
reviews.body: GTA V and the wealth of assets it claims have, quite literally, never been better.

reviews.author: Game Over Online
reviews.body: The driving portion of the game is not as sharp or nuanced as the latest Need for Speed from Criterion. The gunfighting isn’t as crisp or thrilling as nearly any FPS you’d care to mention. There are games that are better at recreating the mechanics of pulling off heists. Yet somehow Rockstar has taken all of these ingots of lead and spun them into gaming gold.

reviews.author: IGN Spain
reviews.body: This is as good as GTA V can get. The new graphical possibilities only enhance its greatness and the possibility of a modding and machinima community lays the foundation to a promising future for the game.

reviews.author: GamingTrend
reviews.body: Grand Theft Auto V on the PC takes an already amazing game and makes it better. Every aspect just seems sharper and better than previous releases. This is how all ports should be handled.

reviews.author: PCGamesN
reviews.body: For those coming fresh to the experience, you’re about to play one of the best games ever made. It’s still very much the Grand Theft Auto you’ve known and loved since the series transitioned to 3D open worlds in GTA3, but it has been enhanced and improved to dizzying heights.

reviews.author: Hooked Gamers
reviews.body: Though it should've come out ages ago, and PC gamers have every right to feel like they were put on the backburner, Rockstar North has tried to make sure that Grand Theft Auto V for PC is the ultimate version. If you ask me overall, they've succeeded.

reviews.author: GameWatcher
reviews.body: You’d think GTA V had been purpose built for the PC, and that’s because it has been – from the generous array of graphics options, to native keyboard and mouse support – Grand Theft Auto V isn’t disguised as a PC game; it is a PC game.

reviews.author: Eurogamer Italy
reviews.body: The already featured-packed game now comes with further improved visuals, a powerful editor and new meaning to the first person mode thanks to the mouse/keyboard combo. Excellence at its finest.

reviews.author: Gamer.nl
reviews.body: After the long wait, PC-gamers get what they have been hoping for. We cannot deny it; the PC is the best way to experience GTA V. Rockstar optimised the game perfectly, and we are happy to start our third and forth play-throughs.

reviews.author: InsideGamer.nl
reviews.body: We have been kept waiting for very long, but GTA 5 for PC really is the best version of the game. It offers the same ingenious story of three characters, plus the online mode with Heists, more music and a very good optimisation. Definitely worth the wait.

reviews.author: LevelUp
reviews.body: Grand Theft Auto V offers a fairly large amount of graphical options to achieve smooth 60 fps on a variety of modest configurations and has enough options to push visual quality up to 4K resolution and beyond for those with beefy enough computers. The arrival of GTA V on PC was long awaited, but it was definitely worth the wait.

reviews.author: PC Gamer
reviews.body: A sandbox of extraordinary scope created with a masterful attention to detail. A patchy campaign doesn't spoil this wonderful, evocative city.

reviews.author: God is a Geek
reviews.body: Look, most of you probably won’t be able to run GTA V in the 4K resolution on Ultra, and most will only dabble in the video editor and director modes. But this is the definitive version of one of the best games in the last few years. What that means is that GTA V on PC remains breathtaking at every corner and was, of course, well worth every minute of the wait.

reviews.author: Atomix
reviews.body: There is no doubt that this is the definitive version of one of the best games in this decade. The Online Heists are guaranteed laughs and the campaign is still perfect as always.

reviews.author: IGN
reviews.body: It’s technically more accomplished in every conceivable way, but it’s also tremendously ambitious in its own right. No other world in video games comes close to this in size or scope, and there is sharp intelligence behind its sense of humour and gift for mayhem...It is one of the very best video games ever made.

reviews.author: Hardcore Gamer
reviews.body: The incredible thing is how alive it all seems.

reviews.author: Twinfinite
reviews.body: A game that will give you faith once again in PC ports. To not take a bite of this seductively colorful cherry does both your rig and your very self a disservice which can never be forgiven.

reviews.author: 4P.de
reviews.body: Visually this is the definitive edition of the gangster drama we all came to love (or hate) with all its gorgeousness as well as its little flaws. And with the editor as well as the director mode creative players get all the tools they need.

reviews.author: GRYOnline.pl
reviews.body: The PC Verizon of GTA V is almost perfect. The superb base version is well known and loved, but the optimization, controls and new options knock it out of the park. Of course there were some issues with the game after the launch - so many delays should have eliminated these kinds of setbacks and for that the final grade goes down a bit. But when you finally get to play GTA V on your PC you'll be amazed.

reviews.author: PC Games
reviews.body: One and a half years after its initial release for last-gen consoles, you’ve probably heard it before: GTA 5 is one of the best games currently out there. So go ahead and buy it already. Rockstar even did a mighty fine job with the PC port.

reviews.author: GameStar
reviews.body: The definitive version of GTA 5 is the PC version. It doesn't just look great, it plays great with the mouse and keyboard.

reviews.author: XGN
reviews.body: Rockstar told us the PC-version would be the ultimate version and they were right. GTA 5 on PC looks incredible and it works almost perfectly. Sadly you might still encounter some bugs and glitches in GTA Online that you already saw on the consoles.

reviews.author: JeuxActu
reviews.body: GTA 5 PC is the best version of Rockstar North's game by a huge margin. Period.

reviews.author: GamesVillage.it
reviews.body: To define GTA V PC as a simple 'porting' is simply wrong: minor technical issues aside (on their way to being fixed), this is the final celebration of a magnificent free-roaming game - perhaps the best ever made. Insanely fluid and beautiful to play.

reviews.author: Guardian
reviews.body: It’s been a long time coming, but Grand Theft Auto V’s PC debut is a triumph...The Rockstar Editor is endlessly entertaining. The online heists are, with friends, some of the most fun you can have in a multiplayer game. The single-player story is an exhilarating series of increasingly absurd missions. And it all takes place in one of the richest, densest, most atmospheric game worlds ever built.

reviews.author: 3DJuegos
reviews.body: GTA V for PC is undoubtedly an essential videogame, including better graphics and every update this far.

reviews.author: Meristation
reviews.body: GTA V represents videogames at their finest. Tense and dramatic when it wants to be, joyful and surprising when the player wants it. Rockstar has managed to harmonize the different trends in the saga, managing to create an unforgettable experience that will be enjoyed for a long time to come. This new version comes with some interesting additions: FPS mode, better graphics, 60 FPS, 2 new radios and the Rockstar Editor.

reviews.author: timon9z
reviews.body: An open-world masterpiece that speaks for itself. The only thing that might surpass it is GTA 6.

reviews.author: Schrutify
reviews.body: Grand Theft Auto V remains an undeniably influential and expansive open-world experience, offering a massive, detailed city that still feels alive years after its release. The three-protagonist structure adds variety to both the narrative and gameplay, allowing the story to explore different perspectives while keeping missions dynamic. Rockstar’s signature attention to detail is evident everywhere, from the world design to the satirical tone that runs through the game’s **** the same time, the experience doesn’t always live up to its full potential. The story, while entertaining, can feel uneven in pacing and tone, sometimes struggling to give equal weight to all three main characters. Certain mission designs lean heavily on linearity, limiting player freedom despite the open-world setting. Additionally, some mechanics and controls feel dated by today’s standards, which can occasionally make gameplay less smooth than expected.Even with these issues, GTA V still offers a highly polished and content-rich experience that’s easy to get lost in. While it may not feel as groundbreaking now as it once did, it remains a solid and enjoyable title that’s worth playing, especially for fans of large-scale open-world games.

reviews.author: LuqDeCastro
reviews.body: O maior produto da história do entretenimento. Gigante em todos os sentidos. Um jogo com um esmero único que só a Rockstar consegue fazer. O trio de personagens me conquistou, uma ótima mudança na série. Não há nada de bom neste jogo que não tenha sido dito.

reviews.author: grindway
reviews.body: estoy cansado jefeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee

reviews.author: Supah69
reviews.body: Really really fun and entertaining game, something random happens every hour and i love it!

reviews.author: User-01
reviews.body: An epic, immersive masterpiece with stunning graphics, compelling story, and endless chaos—GTA V redefines open-world gaming brilliance

reviews.author: Sorcererrr
reviews.body: GTA4 yorumumda gta lardan nefret ettiğimi belirttim ve hala hayranları değilim ancak bu oyun önemli ölçüde yargılarımı kırdı 23 saatte bitirdim bu oyunu eğer biraz daha uzun sürseydi bayardı çünkü gta 4 te 60 saat oynadım aşşırı sıkıldım oda puanımı etkilemiş olabilir ancak bu oyunun hikayesi olsun 23 saatte bitirebilmem olsun ağzımda hoş bir tatla bitti sonu daha güzel olabilirdi C sonunu yapan biri olarak söylüyorum :)

reviews.author: DarkMythHunter
reviews.body: **Grand Theft Auto V – Review (10/10)***Grand Theft Auto V* is easily one of the most ambitious open-world games ever made. From the moment you step into Los Santos, the world feels huge, detailed, and full of things to do. Whether you’re driving through the city, flying over the desert, or exploring the mountains, the map never stops feeling **** of the game’s biggest strengths is its three-character system. Switching between Michael, Franklin, and Trevor keeps the story fresh and unpredictable. Each character has a completely different personality and playstyle, which makes missions more dynamic than in previous GTA **** missions themselves are some of the best in the series, especially the heists. Planning and executing these large-scale robberies feels exciting every time, and the different approaches add replay value. Outside of the story, there’s an endless amount of activities—races, side missions, random encounters, and just pure chaos if you feel like causing trouble.Visually, the game still looks impressive, especially with the level of detail Rockstar put into the world. The soundtrack and radio stations also add a lot of personality while you're driving around Los Santos.Everything comes together to create an experience that’s polished, massive, and consistently fun. *Grand Theft Auto V* takes everything the series is known for and perfects it.**Score: 10/10**

reviews.author: Anirudh-Ratings
reviews.body: First game that I completed 100% including all side quests and missions. All the hype is worth it, story was well driven, combat was possibly the best of its time, great little details, engaging even after the main storyline is finished which is quite rare for a game.

reviews.author: Sepiaak
reviews.body: Literally the most bugged game i have ever played, i hate it. When it rarely works, it can be pretty fun tho.

reviews.author: tollapuZ
reviews.body: Игра выделяется отличной графикой даже в наше время, идеальной физикой, которая хоть и хуже прошлой части, но все же идеальна. Из минусов странные рандомные моменты, когда из любой стороны выскакивает машины и во время вождения всегда врезаешься.

reviews.author: TonyPampooni
reviews.body: People over hate GTA 5 nowadays because of GTA Online. But the story mode is still genuinely phenomenal to play, this game has all the quality of life improvements which the previous games did not have. This game also has the best gameplay out of every GTA game. I do believe that the story was a bit worse than GTA 4's but overall everything else was just done in a more polished way. Plus it had more options for customizations and I liked the dynamic between the three protagonists. Despite the story, this game is still an undeniable masterpiece.

reviews.author: TonyCodesWrong
reviews.body: Being the biggest game of the company, Rockstar North and Dan Houser's Grand Theft Auto V is what we can call an immortal game, not only on the online side, but also on the story. The themes are real and credible. The first time we play as three characters in the GTA's HD Universe. The union of a non-expert street kid, a completely insane and unpredictable man and a retired experienced robber is simply genius by any point of view. Immortal.

reviews.author: hpcGta1212
reviews.body: Nice story(no better), funny, Michael is interesting, Trevor is ok and Franklin lacked charisma, but he is intelligent. GTA 5, in the end, isn't the best GTA(like San andreas, Vice city), but is good

reviews.author: nigro
reviews.body: Online is full of kids and the game is made cheap every mission is the same it was good before 2018

reviews.author: CYBEREBLANIUM
reviews.body: I love this game, I spend a lot time exploring map, in gta online, tried modding too. A tier game in my opinion

reviews.author: bimerfatih
reviews.body:
Artıları 
Michael De Santa, Franklin Clinton ve Trevor Philips arasında anlık geçiş yapmak hem oynanışı çeşitlendiriyor hem hikâyeyi çok daha dinamik hale getiriyor.

Los Santos inanılmaz detaylı. Şehir hayatı, kırsal bölgeler, aktiviteler ve rastgele olaylar sayesinde dünya sürekli canlı hissediyor.

Planlama, hazırlık ve uygulama aşamalarıyla soygun görevleri hâlâ açık dünya oyunlarındaki en iyi görev tasarımlarından biri.

Sürüş, çatışma ve keşif mekanikleri son derece rahat ve keyifli. Oynaması her zaman eğlenceli. 

Yan aktiviteler, mini oyunlar, gizli detaylar… Oyunda yapılacak şey neredeyse bitmiyor.

Rockstar Games ölçek, detay ve teknik performans açısından yine çıtayı yukarı taşımış.

GTA Online faktörünü de unutmamak lazım her ne kadar çok ekmeği yense de :D, Sürekli güncellenen çevrimiçi içerik oyunun ömrünü yıllarca uzattı.
 Eksileri
Büyük ölçekte benim için 10/10 bir oyun.

Bazı oyuncular hikâyenin tonunun daha eğlenceli ve çılgın olmasını, önceki oyunların karanlık havasına göre daha az ciddi bulabiliyor — ama bu bilinçli bir tercih. 

Özgürlük hissi, içerik miktarı ve saf eğlence açısından açık dünya türünün en güçlü temsilcilerinden biri. Yıllar geçse de etkisini kaybetmeyen modern bir klasik.


Puanım: 10/10

reviews.author: Nick_Rostov
reviews.body: Гениальная игра. Невозможно словами описать все чувства и эмоции, которая она подарила. К сожалению, добить на платину не получилось, из-за дурацких онлайн достижений стима.

reviews.author: RdrFanIHateGta
reviews.body: This is game is surely not best game of all time. Not even close. I think Last of Us deserved 2013 Game of the Year. I can say its the best modern time open world. But why would you make three playable characthers? It's annoying. Story is Bad. I don't understand anything about it. I mean. It still a 2013 game. If you gonna play online mod. Buy it. But if you not. Just dont.

reviews.author: Mari_Melo
reviews.body: O GTA mais descartável que existe, são três personagens insuportáveis, o enredo não ajuda, mas o masterpiece de lixo desse jogo é mapa, é o pior mapa de mundo aberto lançado em um game, mapa extremamente entediante ao ponto de me dá sono. É só uma cidade grande para um mapa gigantesco com árvores e morros pra tudo quanto é lado. Nisso o San Andreas é infinitamente melhor, são três cidades grandes bem distribuidas pelo mapa, com pequenas cidades próximas delas e áreas verdes espelhadas.  Já o GTA V eu abomino a ambientação. A jogabilidade é boa e trilha sonora (rádios) é diversificada e só.

reviews.author: JaviManu
reviews.body: Grand Theft Auto V sigue siendo un referente en mundo abierto y narrativa interactiva. La historia de Michael, Franklin y Trevor ofrece una dinámica interesante entre personajes muy distintos, con momentos de humor, tensión y drama que mantienen el **** mundo abierto de Los Santos es enorme, vivo y lleno de detalles. Desde misiones principales hasta actividades secundarias, siempre hay algo que hacer. La conducción, los tiroteos y la libertad para explorar o causar caos siguen siendo el punto más fuerte del juego.Visualmente, el juego envejece bien, y la música y efectos de sonido complementan perfectamente la ambientación de la ciudad. Algunos elementos de repetición en misiones secundarias y ciertas mecánicas ya clásicas pueden sentirse algo anticuadas, pero no afectan gravemente a la experiencia global.Conclusión:un juego imprescindible por su mundo, su narrativa y su libertad, aunque no perfecto, sigue ofreciendo diversión y variedad casi una década después de su lanzamiento.Nota final: 8/10

reviews.author: Favre101
reviews.body: The best GTA game ever. Between the jump in scale of the single player game incorporating multiple playable characters conducting massive heists and the grand scale of the multiplayer that kept it relevant for a decade, this game pushes the boundaries of grand scale open world gameplay in a modern setting. The heists are fun, the side quests are entertaining, and the ability to cause significant chaos like all previous renditions is perfectly crafted for an unforgettable experience solo or with friends.

reviews.author: Polak420
reviews.body: Playing the game since the begenning, from ps3>ps4>ps5 and now pc. The solo and the online are two masterpieces. Hope GTA6 will follow the same direction...

reviews.author: TheWorstCritic
reviews.body: Not that great its a mid game. Its not peak and you should quite has a bad taste in games to disagree with me. Its not the realistic

reviews.author: brenohenrique
reviews.body: Um ótimo jogo da Rockstar Games, com uma ótima história, ambientação, gráficos, personagens e jogabilidade.

reviews.author: iamnotMendes
reviews.body: I'd consider it a masterpiece if you could actually do anything outside of the missions

reviews.author: JuanitoPuntoCom
reviews.body:
Uno de los videojuegos más importantes de la historia y con razón. Aún en 2026 y a las puertas de la próxima entrega, esta sigue siendo una de las mejores experiencias que la industria AAA pudo ofrecer.
Sin duda tiene sus fallos y a veces se le ven un poco las costuras, especialmente a nivel mecánico, sin embargo, en la suma de sus partes encontramos un videojuego excepcional, icónico y divertido.
PD: obviamente me refiero al modo individual.

reviews.author: ibocuks
reviews.body: Overall, the game appealed to me in terms of story length and character relationships. It doesn't feel as realistic as RDR2, but it fully embodies the GTA universe. It's also very open to modding, and has the potential to keep Rockstar financed for at least another 5-6 years, which is why GTA6 hasn't been released yet. The only thing I didn't like was the lack of action in the open world; I hope GTA6 will have more interaction like RDR2.

reviews.author: richmanimtim
reviews.body: i cant believe im writing a grand theft auto v review before grand theft auto vi. but this game GOOD.

reviews.author: bucketpy
reviews.body: The game is very fun and story wise it is interesting. But if you go in online i recommend having friends to goof around. Once one of mine came flying on a motorbike and yelled "ZIMBABWE!," and hit a pole. The open world of this game is amazing. Max graphics makes the game look very nice. This game is a must-have for your collection

reviews.author: Wiiiliax
reviews.body: GTA V, tout simplement LE jeu le plus vendu de tout les temps, et qui semble intuable. après plus d'une décennie, il est toujours au extrènement joué et apprécié. Je crois que c'est l'un des jeux auquel j'ai personnelement le plus joué. La map est juste trop bien réalisé, l'histoire m'a conquis sous tous ses aspects (et plus de trois fois) et ses personnages tout autant ! Je n'ai pas tant joué à GTA Online, mais le concept est géniale (un peu surcoté à mon gout), mais ce jeu regorge de possibilité, entre les mods que l'on peut y installer et tout les serveurs sur Fivem, j'ai passé beaucoup trop de bon moment à Los Santos. Je pense en effet que je devrais juger le jeu uniquement sur le contenu de base, ce qui semble logique vu que ce n'est pas un jeu qui incite au modding (même plutôt l'opposé), et dans ce cas je peux vous dire que j'ai passé des centaines d'heures en solo sur GTA V, sans m'ennuyer, le online, bien que pas trop ma came, est toujours en activité et reçois plusieurs mise à jour et ajout de contenu par ans, ma plus grande déçeption est le manque de contenu additionnel en solo, les possibilitées étaient folles, en therme de DLC solo par exemple, je trouve que ça manque cruellement, et c'aurait pu être géniale (à la sauce GTA IV) mais bon... Il restera un jeu ayant marqué à tout jamais le monde du jeux vidéo et même le monde du divertissement en générale.

reviews.author: MysticMaiden
reviews.body: Despite the early ports from 2015, it's still a masterpiece that reminds me of RDR2 (and also a bit story-wise, looking at the epilogues with John). I love this game; I've played it on Xbox 360, Series X, and twice on PC. I can't tear myself away from the GTA V RP servers; it's forever in my heart.

reviews.author: 4ir4ri
reviews.body: If I were to evaluate *GTA V* itself rather than *GTA Online*, I would give it an exceptionally high score. Not only are the systemic elements—such as freedom and interactivity—impressively well-crafted, but the relationships between characters and the unfolding events are also deeply engaging, making the entire experience highly immersive and enjoyable.

reviews.author: Jakkyx
reviews.body:
Well then... Where do I begin

For such a large open world game, it is aesthetically well-made, I'll give that.

My main problem is actually getting invested into the story, I don't like having to drive or walk around just to do a damn mission

I don't have a problem with Open world games, Breath of the Wild is one of my top Fives, but this game just feels like it makes you travel too much

Not to mention how bland Online is. this part may be subject to bias because I was forced to play with overleveled friends who've invested thousands of hours into this game. But I just found it all really boring, they'd just mess around and say that "you have to get into it" But I cant get into it! If you wanna drive round in vehicles and shoot things, go play Garry's Mod for gods sake...

reviews.author: moussa98
reviews.body:
J'ai adoré l'histoire sur laquelle j'ai passé 120 heures, puis j'ai eu une période gta online ou je j'y jouais tous les jours. Franchement sur le moment le grind était juste hyper satisfaisant et je m'amusais énormément.
Pas 10, car il n'est pas parfait, il ne mérite pas un 10/10. Et ce qui est drôle aussi c'est qu'on passe un temps fou sur ce jeu mais après on se demande ce qu'on a autant aimer et ce qui a fait qu'on y a passé des centaines d'heures.
Mais biensur, son openworld a des défauts mais il reste quand même excellent. De manière générale, tout est qualitatif dans ce jeu. On peut y passer quelques centaines d'heures sans ennuyer surtout avec gta online.

reviews.author: Ali_Akdemir
reviews.body: GTA V hakkında ne diyebilirim bilmiyorum. Oyundaki uçak, helikopter gibi hava araçlarını kullanmak benim için bir hayli zor ve sıkıcıydı. Oyundaki vuruş hissi gayet yerinde. Yan görevler RDR 2 ye kıyasla sıkıcı ben bütün yan görevleri yaptım ve yarısı benim için sıkıcıydı. Oyunun ana görevleri iyi ama bence bu oyunun ana vurucu yeri soyunlar. Soygunların hepsi çok zevkli ve heyecanlı diyebilirim. Oyundaki ana karakterler dışındaki karakterler bana silik ve sıkıcı geldi. Oyunun hikayesi sıradan ve hikaye anlatışı çok bölük pörçük halde. Oyunun sonunda ben C seçeneğini seçtim ve benim oynadığım son gayet iyiydi. Oyun 2013 oyunu olduğunu net hissettiriyor hatta bu yüzden oyunun %70 den sonrasında baya sıkıldım ve oyunu bitirmeye uğraştım. Oyunu oynayıp bitirdikten sonra şunu söyleyebilirim ki oyunun neden online versiyonunun çok fazla oynandığını anladım arkadaşlarınla birlikte soygun düzenlemek kulağa hoş geliyor.

reviews.author: Abcmarj
reviews.body: the gameplay is alright but don't play online mode i hate it those online player try to shoot me for death anyways i love story mode as well? i playing in xbox in 2017and window pc in now mostly

reviews.author: 3vro
reviews.body: There is nothing to say, the only game series that revolutionized the history of gaming

reviews.author: Richiu
reviews.body: One of the best games ever made! It has near-perfect gameplay, very few bugs, and it never gets boring thanks to the huge amount of content. Absolutely amazing — I’m really curious to see what GTA VI will bring to the table!

reviews.author: Kaseta33
reviews.body: zakładam że moge być zaślepione przez nostalgie ponieważ ta gra wyszła w dniach gdzie akurat dostawałem pierwszy playstation które bardzo żałuje ze sprzedałem,wydaje mi się ze jak na 2013 to rewolucja i po prostu nic więcej dodać i ując

reviews.author: ordrigeus
reviews.body: zerei acho que mais de 10 vezes esse jogo, jogar esse jogo me da mais nostalgia doq cheiro de chuva

reviews.author: Swanneee
reviews.body:
Nice game. While it’s not the greatest game for me personally, it’s still a really good one. This is actually the first GTA story mode that I’ve ever finished. I’ve played others like GTA IV and San Andreas before, but I never completed their stories.

Playing GTA V was a lot of fun, there’s so much to do besides the main missions. Honestly, I was shocked to find out that you can actually play the stock market and even watch full cartoons in the game. Like I said, the story mode offers a ton of content. For someone like me who has other things to do, it’s pretty hard to 100% complete it. Even the online mode keeps getting updated constantly.

As for the gameplay, it was definitely ahead of its time. I’ve never played another game that gives you this much freedom. The character movement, the interactions with people, the cars, planes everything feels alive. There are even aliens!

You should also try the online mode. I don’t really play it much, maybe just about 30 minutes of roaming around, killing other players, then getting killed myself… and then I got bored. Maybe playing with some friends would make it more fun. I’ve seen YouTubers and streamers play with their friends, and it honestly looks like a blast.

reviews.author: grallien
reviews.body: Fun game and story and incredibly impressive for its time, but it feels a bit empty from a more modern lens. The predatory business practices do not help.

reviews.author: mazoka
reviews.body: Great game. The graphics, the performance and the shadows were finally improved from IV and pretty much everything else was a lot more detailed... except by the physics. I understand it is more fun to make online races crazy with this arcade driving, but for story mode I would rather the realistic physics from IV. Also, being able to put your case back on it's wheels when it stops upside down is a bummer. I hope they will change this for VI.

reviews.author: RatioExMachina
reviews.body: Kept up with the previous games,added a little more to it even tho i don't like playing as 3 characters once you get used to it you can focus on the individual small stories and the overall grand one

reviews.author: Certified_Poor
reviews.body: Expansive world, gripping story, rich detail, but aging gameplay limits it.

reviews.author: Swannee
reviews.body: The story itself isn’t anything extraordinary, but the characters truly made me feel immersed in the game. Their emotions, actions, and interactions are captivating and fun to follow. Even after finishing the main storyline, the game keeps going with plenty of extra content, random events, activities, and more to explore. As for the online mode, it’s not really my thing, but overall, the experience remains highly enjoyable.

reviews.author: Cologram
reviews.body: Rockstar Editor is the single greatest addition to any game and should be required in every single game.

reviews.author: ArtzWhatever
reviews.body: Grand Theft Auto V is Rockstar at its most ambitious, delivering a massive open world full of personality and things to do. Los Santos is alive with detail, from bustling city streets to the quiet countryside, making exploration endlessly **** three-protagonist structure adds variety and keeps the story fresh, while gameplay balances chaotic action with moments of humor and style. Driving, shooting, and simply messing around in the sandbox all feel engaging and fun.Some dated mechanics and occasional rough edges hold it back from perfection, but GTA V remains one of the most entertaining and replayable open-world games ever made.

reviews.author: Cubed3
reviews.body: Elden Ring is an experience that will not be forgotten in a hurry. It's a tremendous, hulking, massive achievement but because it is tremendous, hulking and massive, it is one that comes with many caveats. The shelves in CeX and conversations with people who stopped playing at Stormveil castle can certainly attest to some of those caveats. Much like any FromSoftware title, it's not going to be for everybody, but paradoxically it is one of their hardest and one of their most accessible to date. It offers huge value in one package, and perhaps is a little too much value, only becoming clearer when nearing the endgame. Don't be mistaken by the criticism, though. This is a phenomenal achievement with a moment-to-moment that is incredibly engaging and immersive. No doubt it will be deserving of the end-of-year accolades it will inevitably receive. One pushes through Elden Ring as one pushes through life, with all the highs and the lows that it brings.

reviews.author: Riot Pixels
reviews.body: Elden Ring is massive, sometimes to its detriment. A thorough walkthrough might take up more than 100 hours to complete, and at some point it becomes clear that From Software did not have enough manpower to produce that much unique content. Still, it remains incredibly addictive even after those 100 hours pass…

reviews.author: RPGamer
reviews.body: It’s hard to review Elden Ring without being comparative; that’s because Elden Ring is an evolutionary step, not a revolutionary one, and one can’t help but see how it’s built on what came before. Here is a massive undertaking of a game, not for the sake of chasing trends but as the natural progression of the style the company has been fostering for over a decade. It’s amazing how smoothly the transition to open world was and how much of the Souls identity is still present. In the short time since its release, it has already left a cultural mark, no doubt a result of the game’s increased approachability. Even after sifting past all the hype and grandiosity, there’s still an excellent game left and Elden Ring is a clear showcase of FromSoftware’s outstanding expertise in the field. Elden Ring really is just that good.

reviews.author: Ragequit.gr
reviews.body: FromSoftware has opened up metaphorically and its bet has paid off. Elden Ring is a solid contender for Game of the Year 2022 despite any technical issues it may have.

reviews.author: KeenGamer
reviews.body: Every once in a while, a game comes around that takes the world by storm, becoming an instant classic. Not only is Elden Ring one such game, it is also one of the most profound among them. FromSoftware’s first foray into the open world genre is nothing short of breathtaking. It effortlessly balances exploration and tough-as-nails action gameplay in a way that has seldom been seen before. While not entirely flawless, it manages to instill a sense of wonder that will make even the most hardened of players feel like a kid again. Although it stumbles, Elden Ring is still leaps and bounds ahead of the competition.

reviews.author: Hooked Gamers
reviews.body: I can't say if Elden Ring will be something genuinely new or more of the same to players who have already battled through the other From Software titles, but playing it felt to me like something I haven't experienced before. I was incredibly stressed, I considered quitting out of frustration several times, but I never stopped having fun and wanting to play just a little bit longer.

reviews.author: MMORPG.com
reviews.body: You will enjoy this game if you can stomach the abuse that Elden Ring dishes out. Even if you cannot, there is so much out there that you can do if you aren't afraid of running from scary fights. Some locations can be less fun than others, and some bosses create skill-based stopgaps. Elden Ring is all about picking yourself up, dusting yourself off, and sometimes finding something more enjoyable to do. It's worth the investment.

reviews.author: GameWatcher
reviews.body: Elden Ring is a walking example of a 10 out of 10 game that isn’t perfect. No game can be perfect but there is just so much in here to love that it becomes hard to pay attention to its mistakes. Every handful of hours had the game open up more and, every time, I was desperate to keep searching, exploring, conquering. Now, over 100 hours later and two playthroughs, all I want is to discover more of what Elden Ring has in store for me. This isn’t just one of the best From Software titles but one of the best games I’ve ever played and I would be shocked if this didn’t have reverberations on the entire games industry.

reviews.author: Merlin'in Kazanı (Turkey)
reviews.body: Despite some technical glitches, Elden Ring has managed to be a satisfying, surprising and fully-experienced game.

reviews.author: Attack of the Fanboy
reviews.body: From Software upped the ante, surpassing even their best efforts that we saw with Sekiro Shadows Die Twice in terms of world building. Elden Ring is an absolute must-play game for 2022, but set aside some time and some patience.

reviews.author: RPG Fan
reviews.body: It’s been my sincerest pleasure experiencing FromSoftware’s latest work. Elden Ring is a landmark title that I hope shapes the future of what this industry can do; maybe we can one day point at this time in 2022 and note that this is when gaming took a giant leap. At risk of “laying it on thick” and coming off as hyperbolic, in my more than 30 years of gaming, I have almost never played a title that so neared perfection. I’ve devoted 80 blissful hours to Elden Ring, and at no point was I not enjoying myself or captivated by this world. My wish is that this team continues to receive the support it deserves and graces us with more opportunities to lose ourselves in the fantastic.

reviews.author: Game Over Online
reviews.body: Dark Souls fans rejoice! Elden Ring is exactly the game you have been hoping it would be, I think, and it is created in grand style. I feel pretty frustrated that the keyboard controls are either so broken or so poorly laid out that I just couldn’t figure them out, and I spent a lot of time trying to (and where some things lie in the menu system are crazy counterintuitive), and I installed it on two different machines to see if it was a hardware problem (note: didn’t help). Elden Ring actually made me want to go back and play Skyrim again, because it always seemed to me that GUI was very well laid out and just made sense. But for you gamepad guys, sharpen up your sword and get in there. You have blood to spill (a lot of which may be your own).

reviews.author: GameSkinny
reviews.body: Almost nothing compares to Elden Ring in quality and depth. This is a game everyone should try at least once if only to get lost in the Lands Between.

reviews.author: Gamers Heroes
reviews.body: While no one wants to call Game of the Year in March, Elden Ring stands at the top of a very tall hill. If you play one game this year, make it Elden Ring.

reviews.author: PCMag
reviews.body: Elden Ring is a wonderful action-RPG that features thrilling combat, an amazing open world, and tons of replay value. Unfortunately, its PC performance needs work.

reviews.author: GameGrin
reviews.body: ELDEN RING is the most accessible entry to a FromSoftware title to date, with plenty of things to explore, do, and enjoy. Those even mildly interested by the premise should give it a shot.

reviews.author: AusGamers
reviews.body: Elden Ring is among my favourite games ever. Those frame hitches, while significant, marred mere moments in my playtime with From's masterpiece. I played 95 hours of Elden Ring, and I could have played oh-so-much more. I will, probably. I railroaded myself with my weapon choice, but I found dozens of amazing looking spells that I'd love to try out. And I can't PVP at level 150, so I'll need to start a new character if I want to invade people. And there are two other endings that I didn't achieve — you aren't thrust immediately into NG+ in Elden Ring once you achieve an ending, but I don't think you can achieve other endings later. I already kind of know how to do them, I think. Well, I think I know, anyway. But I don't know what I don't know, so…

reviews.author: IGN Italia
reviews.body: Elden Ring is an incredible game that takes the "soulslike" formula and declines it within a vast and dense world full of elements arranged with extreme care. It is a demanding experience, which requires the player's focus and a high desire to explore and experiment: it gives the best of itself when the player is ready to explore the huge Lands Between. Its world design has made the structures of the previous soulslike games obsolete. Simply a new milestone for its genre.

reviews.author: Stevivor
reviews.body: Overall, Elden Ring is slightly held back by some lacking information in the UI, the uneven nature of the difficulty towards the endgame, and the inconsistent performance on the PC platform. Despite this, it's an incredible experience and will be one of the best games you’ll play this year.

reviews.author: Everyeye.it
reviews.body: Elden Ring is the sum of FromSoftware's experience, poetics and playful approach: a title in which the undisputed skills of the team converge and explode.

reviews.author: Siliconera
reviews.body: Elden Ring is a potentially great game that is hindered by massive performance issues. It doesn’t do anything particularly genre-defining, as it mostly sticks to what FromSoftware always does. It will no doubt please fans of the Dark Souls series. But its lack of accessibility options and somewhat restrictive multiplayer may leave some wanting. Ultimately, I think it will potentially offer a better experience down the line, as the day-one patch has already changed some fundamentals of the game. With no real improvement regarding its performance on PC, Elden Ring is a hard game to recommend until a more stable version is available.

reviews.author: PC Games
reviews.body: Elden Ring absolutely manages to deliver on what was promised, and is, despite some minor annoyances, one of the finest gaming experiences I’ve had in years. That’s not only due to the rock-solid gameplay foundation, with its flexible class system, versatile weapons and progression mechanics. It’s also not only because of the morbid, dense atmosphere and the mysterious, captivating setting. It isn’t even solely the open world, which makes me want to spend every spare second exploring it with its gorgeous, monumental vistas and densely packed locations. Instead, the masterful combination of all these great qualities is what really makes Elden Ring special. Yes, the game has stopped me in my tracks multiple times, forcing me to leave bosses and places for later due to the somewhat slower progression system. But every time it does, it offers up dozens of other locations to test my mettle, become stronger and more skilled. And this cycle of trial and triumph is so immensely engaging that it’s going to keep me entertained for months to come.

reviews.author: LevelUp
reviews.body: Elden Ring is everything we dream of and much more. It is the natural evolution of a franchise that has captivated several generations and is now ready to capture many more. Elden Ring will be the most talked about game of the next decade. It's proof that FromSoftware is a master of its craft and a testament that it dominates the genre it has created. Those who try to do something similar will remain mere imitators: they will never reach the level of sophistication achieved in Elden Ring.

reviews.author: GameSpace
reviews.body: A gorgeous and wonderful open world adventure full of discovery, magic, and the same threat that exemplifies FromSoftware's Souls series. Elden Ring is more than another Dark Souls Game, mixing a natural evolution of the series with an open world concept and a greater level of accessibility for those that want to experience the infamous You Died screen.

reviews.author: But Why Tho?
reviews.body: Elden Ring is an absolute triumph that will surely please all of the many players eager to get their hands on it.

reviews.author: Checkpoint Gaming
reviews.body: The hype behind Elden Ring was always going to be a challenge to match, yet the game succeeds in almost every way. This detailed and exciting world is full of wondrous moments and brutal fights as the excellent Souls gameplay finds itself being utilised in a freshly open-world format. The variety of ways you can approach combat and the sheer volume of viable tools you can use to take down gruesome and frightening foes makes for an experience that never gets old. A game unwavering in its vision, Elden Ring stands out as a monumental 2022 release.

reviews.author: ComicBook
reviews.body: Elden Ring isn't a perfect FromSoftware game, but it's a remarkable evolution of nearly everything people loved or detested about the developer's past works. It's found what feels like an agreeable balance between accommodating newcomers and alleviating frustrations while maintaining the signature difficulty expected from the studio.

reviews.author: GameByte
reviews.body: If Dark Souls was the earthquake, Elden Ring is the aftershock. In a sense, it’s more of a love letter to the Soulsborne fanbase, incorporating dramatic fragments of each past game into a single product that is rewarding and fulfilling. While not perfect, I believe that FromSoftware has followed through on the hype surrounding this game and been able to create something truly mesmerising.

reviews.author: GameSpot
reviews.body: In a genre that has become wrought with bloated and over-designed games, Elden Ring is defiantly contrarian in almost every way. Its commitment to design by subtraction and to placing the responsibility of charting a path through its world entirely on the player makes it stand head and shoulders above other open-world titles. Elden Ring takes the shards of what came before and forges them into something that will go down in history as one of the all-time greats: a triumph in design and creativity, and an open-world game that distinguishes itself for what it doesn't do as much as what it does.

reviews.author: Hardcore Gamer
reviews.body: Elden Ring is one of the best games in years, with a breathtaking vast world to explore, and one of the most satisfying combat systems in an RPG. This is the accumulation of FromSoftware’s work over the last thirteen years and an evolution on its inspiring formula.

reviews.author: Hey Poor Player
reviews.body: Naysayers will point to reused assets, a lowering of the difficulty in Spirit Summons, but these people miss the point. Elden Ring is the definitive Souls experience, introducing a class-leading open-world design and mechanics that will draw more people to the franchise. And that’s nothing but a good thing.

reviews.author: IGN France
reviews.body: Elden Ring is the apex of the Soulsborne formula, but if you thought this instance will be easier to play and to beat, you’ll have to put your foolish ambitions to rest. The adventure in the Land-Between open world is a work of art and definitely unforgettable.

reviews.author: MGG
reviews.body: Elden Ring manages to take everything that makes Dark Souls games stand out, and then apply it on a much larger scale, in a huge and complex world. The exploration and combat are rich, challenging and satisfying, and there's a constant sense of new things to discover everywhere. But many shortcuts have been taken. The game has some flaws and technical limitations, such as shaky multiplayer and a sometimes frustrating camera. Without being an absolute revolution, Elden Ring offers a unique vision of open world gaming, and it stands as a new model of the genre.

reviews.author: Noisy Pixel
reviews.body: Although challenging and grand, Elden Ring brilliantly immerses players in its fantasy world. The game rewards every type of player, from the curious explorer to the action-hungry warrior; you'll find something to love about this game. Even after 30 hours, there are still many adventures that await me in The Lands Between. This is an evolution of every Soulslike game that came before, and although brutally challenging, I couldn't put it down for a second.

reviews.author: Shacknews
reviews.body: Simply put, Elden Ring is Miyazaki and FromSoftware’s magnum opus. It feels like the vision for the genre has reached its final form. Elden Ring is a meticulously crafted game, with a world begging to be explored and experienced. It is truly mesmerizing in its scope, an astonishing success and everything I had hoped it would be. Forget what you knew about the genre, the real Soulsborne starts here, with Elden Ring.

reviews.author: Twinfinite
reviews.body: As an open-world skeptic who was pretty much convinced that the Souls formula wouldn't work in such a setting, I'm happy to have been proven wrong. Elden Ring retains the heart of what makes FromSoft's action RPGs so compelling, and takes things to a whole other level by pulling the best elements from their older games and improving upon them in this one.

reviews.author: Wccftech
reviews.body: Elden Ring is not only the best action RPG developed by From Software but one of the best open-world titles ever made. A masterclass of game design, Elden Ring isn't just a game but an epic dark fantasy journey that no one will be able to forget for a very long time.

reviews.author: We Got This Covered
reviews.body: Elden Ring is a marvel with scope and diversity of mind-boggling proportions. At the risk of hyperbole, it has dethroned Bloodborne as my favorite game of all time. It's all I've been able to think about since I got my hands on it, and the only thing I want to do now is play it with the rest of you.

reviews.author: WellPlayed
reviews.body: Backed by an incredible and rewarding open-world design, Elden Ring is a masterclass in every facet that it brings to the table. Without a doubt, this is FromSoftware’s magnum opus.

reviews.author: ZTGD
reviews.body: While the technical issues of the barebones PC port and the familiar trappings of the open world formula substantially soured my experience, Elden Ring is still an impressive achievement of fusing compelling world design, exploration and combat.

reviews.author: PCGamesN
reviews.body: A supercut of FromSoft's very best work, transported to a totally new space. Elden Ring is not only a masterpiece by its developer's lofty standards, but in packing so much density into the Lands Between, it lights the path ahead for open-world games in general.

reviews.author: Trusted Reviews
reviews.body: Elden Ring is a fantastic RPG for any hardcore gamer looking for a new world to explore. Combat is weighty and takes place in a wonderfully intriguing world full of dungeons to explore and monsters to battle. The only downside is that its high difficulty will be a stumbling block that will continue to put some players off, despite the improved accessibility that comes with the open-world format.

reviews.author:
tbd
Rock, Paper, Shotgun
reviews.body: That's the thing about Elden Ring, its open world has your back, in its own - often painful - way. Souls has always been about inching through claustrophobic corridors and bashing your skull against whatever's in the way. You compare foreheads with friends, "Look at this bruise Barry, that's me five hours in". And Barry would probably peel his hair back and reveal the exact same bruise. Adventures in past Souls games tend to line up, as you're all wandering down the same pathways. But that's not the case with Elden Ring. You might show your bruise to Barry and he'll peel his hair back to reveal a scar or a massive indent or a tattoo of a mouse tustling with a skeleton. Both of may be five hours in but you'll be on totally different trajectories. No matter how hostile the game's world may be, it pushes you to succeed and carve out your own adventures. It is, by far, the most encouraging Souls yet. [RPS Bestest Bests]

reviews.author: Game Informer
reviews.body: Elden Ring challenged me, captivated me, and enchanted me, an unyielding deluge of discovery and artistic vision unbound. Elden Ring represents a truly amazing combination of various game elements that all come together to create something fascinating, special, and unforgettable. Elden Ring isn’t just the best game this year; it’s one of the best games ever made.

reviews.author: PC Gamer
reviews.body: An open world action RPG from FromSoftware that reaches new heights, but spends too much time in the familiar.

reviews.author: VG247
reviews.body: What ultimately matters, however, is that Elden Ring succeeds at almost every goal it sets out to achieve. It’s the culmination of years of refinement of FromSoftware’s formula. Mechanically, and thematically, this is a game making a statement: that you can buck industry tendencies even as you adopt their trends.

reviews.author: Jeuxvideo.com
reviews.body: With its new open-world dimension, Elden Ring brilliantly fleshes out FromSoftware's already highly effective formula and offers Souls a spiritual sequel that is both ambitious and accessible. All the new features converge towards this last objective: the open world allows you to arm yourself more than ever with the epic and obligatory fights that the adventure reserves, without having the impression of walking around just to farm. And the best part is that it doesn't compromise the sense of accomplishment so important to the genre. Elden Ring is a complete, mysterious experience, where curiosity is tirelessly renewed by the feeling of discovering something for the first time. All with a sumptuous artistic direction, which makes you want to get lost, to explore. The title also welcomes new features that offer strategic choices in combat, without betraying their requirement. We only regret a slight lack of ambition for certain Legacy Dungeons, as well as the recycling of some assets and bosses. Overall, an exceptional title.

reviews.author: Easy Allies
reviews.body: Any new From Software game comes with a lot of expectations, and Elden Ring delivers. It's a thrilling adventure that provides a wonderful sense of discovery through an expertly crafted world, seamlessly incorporating the excellent combat and encounters the studio is known for. The level of difficulty may be off-putting for some, but Elden Ring is so fantastic that it's absolutely worth fighting through to the end.

reviews.author: NME
reviews.body: FromSoftware’s most ambitious title yet, Elden Ring is a glowing spectacle of achievement. A refined open-world format adds unimagined wonder to the game, whilst legacy dungeons will likely go down as some of FromSoftware’s best level design. Beyond that, there are so many improvements to the Souls formula that make this an essential play for fans – this is a game that brings so much to the table, yet somehow leaves nothing out.

reviews.author: IGN
reviews.body: Elden Ring is a massive iteration on what FromSoftware began with the Souls series, bringing its relentlessly challenging combat to an incredible open world that gives us the freedom to choose our own path.

reviews.author: Mrs_john8
reviews.body: Jogo maravilhoso, além de por si só ser incrivel, tem uma rejogabilidade maravilhosa perfeito, bosses realmente dificeis mas muito bem feitos, historia mt solida, o que ferra é o boss final, facilmente o pior boss final de todos os soulsborn.

reviews.author: patdez
reviews.body:
Elden Ring is the culmination of everything From Software has done over the past decade-plus. The combat is refined, difficult, and rewards players who are willing to learn the systems. The world is truly awe-inspiring with bosses that fit the atmosphere seamlessly. Whenever you think you have seen the entire map, you stumble upon another area, and another, until you realize you are playing a game of such massive scale and grandeur that you have to put the controller down to digest it all. The soundtrack is beautiful and is so important to this game because it sets the mood for every area and boss fight. Walking through Weeping Peninsula with the Limgrave soundtrack is going to be what I remember on my deathbed. Contrast the soundtrack for Lichdragon Fortissax with that of Ancestral Spirit, two entirely different fights whose music matches the moment. The scale of exploration, combined with soulsborne combat, made for a first playthrough unlike any other.

Countless weapons and spells lead to a wide swath of playstyles that the player can choose from. The game does not give you anything overpowered from the get go but, with exploration and determination, you can obtain gear that matches the challenge that The Lands Between presents. Most of the armaments are viable and with many weapon types, there is almost too much to try. 

The bosses are the pinnacle of From's boss design philosophy. These bosses are no longer those of Dark Souls 1 through 3 which give you fairly large and obvious punish and healing windows. Instead, the windows are tightened and you are forced to learn each moveset to succeed, there are few lucky or cheesy victories to be had in Elden Ring. Elden Ring also emphasizes status effects and poise buildup, giving the player more of a reward for playing aggressive. Not all of the bosses are incredibly difficult, but those that are are thoughtfully designed and are unparalleled in games history. The setting and music during the Mohg fight, combined with the difficulty of the fight, make it one of the best From has ever concocted. Malenia was infuriating at first but once you get it, its one of the most fun fights in any game. I wish ER had a boss rush mode for this fight alone. Morgott's fight is easier but man, what a tragic character he is and his curved sword is the coolest weapon in the game. 

Elden Ring makes good games look like bad games. In the words of Dunkey- "if you can play this game and not feel some kind of emotion come over you, wipe yourself off man, because you are dead.

reviews.author: jun201010
reviews.body: From the moment you step out into the sprawling, golden-hued vista of Limgrave, Elden Ring makes one thing abundantly clear: this is not just a game; it is a monumental achievement in interactive storytelling and world-building. Developed by FromSoftware and directed by the visionary Hidetaka Miyazaki, in collaboration with George R.R. Martin, Elden Ring represents the pinnacle of the "Soulslike" genre, transcending its predecessors to become a universal masterpiece.A World of Boundless WonderThe Lands Between is perhaps the most meticulously crafted open world in gaming history. Unlike many contemporary titles that clutter the map with repetitive icons, Elden Ring thrives on organic discovery. Every ruined tower, shimmering Erdtree, and jagged cliffside tells a story. The sense of scale is staggering, yet the world feels intimate, filled with secrets that reward the curious and the brave. Whether you are descending into the star-lit depths of Siofra River or navigating the gothic nightmare of Stormveil Castle, the art direction is consistently **** Dance of Combat and ChallengeThe gameplay remains the gold standard for high-stakes combat. It is rigorous and demanding, yet fairer than ever before. The introduction of "Spirit Ashes" and a more flexible magic system allows players to tailor their journey to their own playstyle without sacrificing the profound sense of accomplishment that comes from overcoming a formidable boss. Each encounter with a "Shardbearer" feels like a mythic event—a desperate, cinematic struggle against gods and monsters that tests both skill and resolve.A Narrative of Mythic ProportionsThe lore of Elden Ring is a rich tapestry of tragedy, ambition, and cosmic horror. Instead of forceful exposition, the story is whispered through item descriptions, environmental cues, and the cryptic pleas of its tragic inhabitants. It invites the player to be an archeologist of a fallen world, piecing together the fractured history of the Golden Order.ConclusionElden Ring is a rare triumph that manages to live up to its immense hype. It captures the essence of adventure—the fear of the unknown, the thrill of discovery, and the glory of triumph. It is a hauntingly beautiful experience that stays with you long after the screen goes dark, solidifying its place as one of the greatest video games ever made. It is, quite simply, an essential experience for anyone who loves the medium of art.

reviews.author: Favre101
reviews.body: A difficult, beautifully crafted experience. Fromsoft is not normally known for their open world style game creation, but they find a great way to blend their intense combat style with the exploration of an open world. The bosses and mobs are maybe not Fromsoft's best, but are largely well crafted for this new style of world. Though the open world story and exploration is maybe not as up to par with other top tier games, this game is easily a Game of the Year worthy contender with a repeatably playable experience.

reviews.author: talles_souza49
reviews.body: Elden Ring is absolutely insane in the best possible way. FromSoftware took the Souls formula and unleashed it into a massive, hostile, and breathtaking open world where every corner hides danger, mystery, and reward. The game doesn’t hold your hand, doesn’t go easy on you, and never apologizes for it it challenges you to learn, explore, and improve through persistence. Every victory feels earned, every defeat teaches a lesson, and every boss you overcome delivers a rush few games can **** combat is heavy, precise, and brutal, while the world and its lore are told in a subtle, intelligent, and deeply engaging way. Visually and artistically, Elden Ring is striking from start to finish, with epic landscapes and perfectly timed music. It’s not just a difficult game it’s an experience that respects the player and redefines what an action RPG can be. Elden Ring is unforgettable.

reviews.author: FulanoPersona
reviews.body: It feels like an honor to be alive to experience Elden Ring. It is not hyperbole to say this may be the greatest game ever made.

reviews.author: Elsendreta3
reviews.body: Facil y rapido, sencillamente, una bestialidad de juego y un GOTY indiscutible. Su punto fuerte es todo lo que no tiene que ver con la narrativa directa: el mundo está increíblemente vivo y el mapa es tan absurdamente grande que te recompensa por cada rincón que exploras. La libertad es total, permitiéndote perderte por horas mientras descubres que las posibilidades de builds son infinitas: desde magias espectaculares hasta ballestas, escudos y armas con habilidades únicas que te dejan con la boca abierta. El apartado de jefe vs. jugador es magnífico. Las peleas ganan una seriedad y una épica brutales gracias a una de las mejores bandas sonoras que se han escuchado nunca, que te mete de lleno en el combate. Además, aunque use el motor de Dark Souls 3, la ambientación y el diseño artístico hacen que los gráficos se defiendan de lujo. Lo mejor es que la dificultad es muy ajustable: si quieres sufrir vas solo, y si no, las invocaciones están ahí, haciendo que sea un juego recomendado para todo el mundo, aunque no te gusten los Souls. La única pega es que la historia es muy floja y no se entiende nada mientras juegas, pero con un mundo tan completo, es un 9,4 fácil.

reviews.author: Diego_Brando
reviews.body:
I completed the game 100% and was satisfied, as I did not expect a game in a genre that was new to me to be so interesting. I am deeply grateful to the developers for their outstanding work: the graphics, combat system, bosses, and lore are all top-notch, and the game world is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful in the gaming industry. Unfortunately, due to my lack of previous experience with this genre, I was somewhat disappointed with the plot and side quests, although the lore is extremely interesting and rich, which, as I understand it, is a characteristic feature of the developer. I recently purchased Sekiro and plan to complete it 100% by the end of 2026.

Verdict: "One of the best," but subjectively does not achieve the status of the absolute best. However, for me, it will definitely be in my top 10 favorite games.

reviews.author: ChoportXGaming
reviews.body: Elden Ring is the closest gaming has ever come to perfection. The world is massive, mysterious, and beautifully designed, rewarding exploration in every direction. Every boss fight feels unique and memorable, and the freedom to build your character however you want makes every playthrough different. The atmosphere, music, and lore combine into something unforgettable. No microtransactions, no hand-holding - just pure, masterful game design. Elden Ring isn’t just a game, it’s an experience that stays with you long after the credits. For me, this is the best game ever made.
